zh.lifeSummary: 當Clarice de La Warre於1280年出生在英國素可易斯費爾德時，她的父親Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield11277歲，母親Clarice de Tregoz18歲。她於1306年1月12日與Adam Everingham在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾內瓦爾可--特倫特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1321年8月25日在英國薩默塞特康普頓 馬丁享年41歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾拉可頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Clarice de La Warre于1280年出生在英国素可易斯費爾德时，她的父亲Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield11277岁，母亲Clarice de Tregoz18岁。她于1306年1月12日与Adam Everingham在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾內瓦爾可--特倫特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1321年8月25日在英国薩默塞特康普頓 馬丁享年41岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾拉可頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English:  see  Warr  .
2  English:  see  Ware  .

variants.name: Warr
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a warrior, usually from Norman French were, werre, waire, warre (central French guerre) ‘war, battle’, and short for de la werre (de la guerre, see Delaware ), literally ‘(man) of the war’, a construction paralleled in de la Bataille (see Battle ). Occasionally it is a shortened form of Old French werreur ‘warrior’.
English: variant of   Ware  .

variants.name: Ware
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by or looked after a dam or weir on a river (from Old English wær, wer, Middle English war(e), warre, wer(e), werre ‘weir’), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Weare (Somerset), Weare Giffard (Devon), or Ware (Hertfordshire).
topographic name for someone who lived by a horse pond or fish pond, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English wayour, which became early modern English ware, wear.

nameMeanings.name: Clarice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval English and French form of the Latin name Claritia. This may have meant ‘fame’ (an abstract derivative of clārus ‘famous’), but as a given name it may have been no more than an arbitrary elaboration of Clara . It was borne by a character who features in some versions of the medieval romances of Roland and the other paladins of Charlemagne, and in recent times by the ceramic artist Clarice Cliff ( 1899–1972 ).

variants.name: Claris
meanings: Medieval English and French form of the Latin name Claritia. This may have meant ‘fame’ (an abstract derivative of clārus ‘famous’), but as a given name it may have been no more than an arbitrary elaboration of Clara . It was borne by a character who features in some versions of the medieval romances of Roland and the other paladins of Charlemagne, and in recent times by the ceramic artist Clarice Cliff ( 1899–1972 ).

variants.name: Clarisse
meanings: Medieval English and French form of the Latin name Claritia. This may have meant ‘fame’ (an abstract derivative of clārus ‘famous’), but as a given name it may have been no more than an arbitrary elaboration of Clara . It was borne by a character who features in some versions of the medieval romances of Roland and the other paladins of Charlemagne, and in recent times by the ceramic artist Clarice Cliff ( 1899–1972 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Clarice de La Warre was born in 1280, in Isfield, Sussex, England, her father, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, was 11277 and her mother, Clarice de Tregoz, was 18. She married Adam Everingham on 12 January 1306, in Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 25 August 1321, in Compton Martin, Somerset, England, at the age of 41, and was buried in Laxton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Clarice de La Warre est née en 1280, à Isfield, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, avait 11277 ans et sa mère, Clarice de Tregoz, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Adam Everingham le 12 janvier 1306, à Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 25 août 1321, à Compton Martin, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterrée à Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Clarice de La Warre 1280 in Isfield, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield 11277 und ihre Mutter Clarice de Tregoz 18. Sie hat am 12. Januar 1306 in Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Adam Everingham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 25. August 1321 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Compton Martin, Somerset, England und wurde in Laxton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Clarice de La Warre è nata il nel 1280 nel Isfield, Sussex, England, sua padre, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, aveva 11277 anni e sua madre, Clarice de Tregoz, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Adam Everingham il 12 gennaio 1306, nel Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 25 agosto 1321, nel Compton Martin, Somerset, England, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolta a Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Clarice de La Warreが1280年にイギリススセックスイスフィールドで生まれた時，父Roger la Warre Lord of Isefieldは11277歳，母Clarice de Tregozは18歳であった。1306年1月12日にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアニューアルク-オン-トレントでAdam Everinghamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1321年8月25日にイギリスソメルセトコンプトン マルティンで41歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアラクストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Clarice de La Warre(이)가 1280년 영국 수세스 이스필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield(은)는 11277세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Clarice de Tregoz(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1306년 1월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 뉴아르k--트렌트에서 Adam Everingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1321년 8월 25일 영국 서머세트 콤프턴 마틴에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 라스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Clarice de La Warre nasceu em 1280, em Isfield, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, tinha 11277 anos e sua mãe, Clarice de Tregoz, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Adam Everingham em 12 de janeiro de 1306, em Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 25 de agosto de 1321, em Compton Martin, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 41 anos, e foi sepultada em Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Clarice de La Warre родилась в 1280 г. в местности исфиелд, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield было 11277 лет, а матери, которую звали Clarice de Tregoz, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Adam Everingham 12 января 1306 г. в местности нюарк-он-трент, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 25 августа 1321 г. в местности комптон мартинь, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 41 год и была похоронена в местности лакстон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Clarice de La Warre nació en 1280, en Isfield, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, tenía 11277 años y su mamá, Clarice de Tregoz, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Adam Everingham el 12 de enero de 1306, en Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 25 de agosto de 1321, en Compton Martin, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultada en Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
